name: Growth Hormone, IGF, and Insulin Axis Attenuation
summary: Intervention family intended to reduce GH receptor, IGF-1, IGF1R, or insulin-linked growth signaling; supported strongly by animal genetics and human natural experiments but not yet by aging-directed human intervention trials.
mechanism_summary: Attempts to dampen somatotropic and insulin/IGF-linked growth signaling, lowering IGF-1, insulin receptor tone, or downstream Akt/Foxo pathway activity; human translation is constrained by developmental effects, tissue specificity, repair biology, and endocrine safety.

open_questions: Whether adult-onset partial GH/IGF attenuation can reproduce animal benefits without developmental dwarfism or frailty tradeoffs
Which tissue, sex, and baseline endocrine or metabolic states would benefit versus be harmed
Whether human natural-experiment disease protection can be translated into an intentional preventive therapy
